input.topic: Impact of remote work on productivity

sources.title: Stanford Remote Work Study 2023
sources.content: A 9-month study of 1,600 workers found remote workers were 13% more productive than office-based peers. Productivity gains came from fewer breaks, shorter meetings, and more focused work time.

sources.title: McKinsey Workplace Report
sources.content: Survey of 25,000 professionals showed 58% reported equal or higher productivity working remotely. However, 32% noted challenges with collaboration and brainstorming. Hybrid models showed the best overall outcomes.

sources.title: Nature Human Behaviour Study
sources.content: Analysis of 60,000 Microsoft employees found remote work reduced cross-group collaboration by 25% and increased asynchronous communication. Innovation metrics showed a modest decline in new patent filings.

sources.title: IEA Carbon Capture Report 2024
sources.content: Current carbon capture and storage (CCS) facilities capture approximately 45 Mt CO2 per year globally. The IEA projects this needs to reach 1,200 Mt by 2030 for net-zero targets. Current capture rates average 85-90% from flue gas.

sources.title: Environmental Science Review
sources.content: Meta-analysis of 20 CCS projects found average capture efficiency of 78%, significantly below stated targets. Cost overruns averaged 50% above initial estimates. Three of the 20 projects were abandoned due to technical failures.

sources.title: Carbon Capture Industry Brief
sources.content: Next-generation direct air capture (DAC) facilities demonstrate 95%+ capture rates in pilot programs. Costs have fallen from $600/ton to $250/ton since 2020, with projections of $100/ton by 2030 at scale.

sources.title: Martin Fowler - Microservices Guide
sources.content: Microservices enable independent deployment, technology diversity, and organizational scalability. Key benefits include fault isolation, independent scaling, and team autonomy. However, they introduce distributed system complexity.

sources.title: Google DORA Report 2023
sources.content: Teams using microservices with strong CI/CD practices deployed 208x more frequently than low performers. However, without proper observability, microservices increased incident resolution time by 40%.

sources.title: Cochrane Review 2023
sources.content: Meta-analysis of 81 RCTs (n=53,000) found vitamin D supplementation reduced risk of respiratory infections by 12% (RR 0.88, 95% CI 0.81-0.96). Effect was significant only in individuals with baseline deficiency (<25 nmol/L).

sources.title: NEJM Vitamin D Trial
sources.content: Large RCT (n=25,000) found no significant effect of vitamin D supplementation on cardiovascular events (HR 0.96, p=0.30) or cancer incidence (HR 0.99, p=0.75) over 5-year follow-up.

sources.title: The Lancet Diabetes Endocrinology
sources.content: Mendelian randomization study (n=100,000) suggests causal effect of vitamin D on multiple sclerosis risk, but not on type 2 diabetes or depression. Observational studies likely overestimate benefits due to confounding.

sources.title: AI Safety Benchmark 2024
sources.content: Evaluation of 12 major LLMs on factual accuracy found hallucination rates ranging from 3% to 27% depending on domain. Medical and legal domains showed highest hallucination rates. Models with retrieval augmentation reduced hallucinations by 60%.
